featured.title: Jewish Museum Berlin: Free Entry, How Long and What to See (2026)
featured.label: Newest free guide
featured.summary: A practical first-visit guide to the Jewish Museum Berlin: free entry, how long to allow, what to see first, security, accessibility and a route through Libeskind's building.
featured.alt: Baroque Old Building and zinc-clad Libeskind Building of the Jewish Museum Berlin seen from Lindenstrasse.

miniPosts.title: Mauerpark on a Sunday: Berlin's Best Free Flea Market and Karaoke (2026)
miniPosts.summary: A Berlin guide's take on Mauerpark on a Sunday: the free flea market, the open-air Bearpit Karaoke, the best times to arrive, and how to shape a Berlin Sunday around it.
miniPosts.alt: A large crowd fills the stone amphitheatre at Mauerpark for the Sunday Bearpit Karaoke, with the green park behind them

miniPosts.title: Does Uber Work in Berlin? Ride-Hailing Apps Explained (2026)
miniPosts.summary: Yes, Uber works in Berlin, but not the way it does at home. A local guide to Uber, Bolt and FreeNow: how ride-hailing really works here, what it costs, and when the train beats the app.
miniPosts.alt: Berlin Brandenburg Airport Terminal 1 at dusk, with the long glass frontage and the curbside pickup lane where ride apps and taxis wait

miniPosts.title: Air Conditioning in Berlin: Do Hotels Have It, and How to Stay Cool (2026)
miniPosts.summary: A Berlin guide's honest take on air conditioning in Berlin: why so few places have it, how to check before you book, and how to keep a no-AC room liveable in a summer heatwave.
miniPosts.alt: A classic Berlin Altbau room in summer with two tall open casement windows, sheer curtains, potted plants on the sill, an old cast-iron radiator, herringbone parquet floor and a small white electric fan by the window, warm afternoon sunlight across the floor

miniPosts.title: Berlin Wall Memorial (Bernauer Strasse): How to Visit the Real Wall Site (2026)
miniPosts.summary: A practical guide to the Berlin Wall Memorial on Bernauer Strasse: the preserved death strip, the viewing tower, the ghost station and the Window of Remembrance.
miniPosts.alt: The preserved Berlin Wall death strip on Bernauer Strasse seen from the viewing tower, with the TV Tower on the skyline

posts.title: Berlin U-Bahn Fine: What Happens If You Are Caught Without a Ticket
posts.summary: A practical Berlin U-Bahn fine guide for tourists caught without a valid ticket: €60 EBE, unstamped paper tickets, wrong zones, forgotten passes and official next steps.
posts.alt: Yellow U-Bahn train at Prinzenstrasse station in Berlin

posts.title: Kaiser Wilhelm Memorial Church: How to Visit Berlin's Broken Tower (2026)
posts.summary: Berlin's hollow tooth is free, quick, and easy to miss the point of. A local guide to visiting the Kaiser Wilhelm Memorial Church: the two interiors, the hours that trip people up, and when to go.
posts.alt: The blue-lit modern church and the floodlit broken old tower of the Kaiser Wilhelm Memorial Church at night on Breitscheidplatz

posts.title: Where to Watch the 2026 World Cup in Berlin (No, There's No Brandenburg Gate Fan Mile This Year)
posts.summary: The 2026 World Cup is in North America, and Berlin has no Brandenburg Gate fan mile this year. Here is where to actually watch: free screens, beer gardens and bars, with every kick-off in Berlin time.
posts.alt: A summer evening football public viewing in Berlin with a relaxed crowd, outdoor screen and TV Tower skyline
